note: Composition-pinning ledger for WIN-FND-007. It pins the CURRENT, real Windows icon-theme selection / package-naming / fallback contract and enumerates icon-themes/ to record an honest material_theme_installed:false. The Material Start Center glyph set (see material_startcenter_glyphs) is the first Material icon SOURCE shipped -- 18px monochrome SVGs authored under colibre/colibre_svg plus links.txt reuse-aliases wiring the sfx2/res/startcenter/* icon-names startcenter.ui references -- so the mega-wave blanket 'no Material icon assets exist yet' claim is INVERTED for exactly that set (now required-present). No Material icon THEME is installed, so material_theme_installed stays false and no material* theme directory exists. Beyond that set this still fail-closed pins what is real today (the hardcoded Windows colibre fallback, the generic mPreferredIconTheme override that lets any correctly-named installed theme win, the images_<id>[_dark][_svg].zip naming any future material package must satisfy, the literal 20-directory icon-themes/ set with no material* directory, and the four qualitative iconography prose rules) so future drift is caught. Source/text evidence only; runtime_verified:false; no build, icon pixels, RTL-mirroring, or scaling evidence is claimed and no gate is moved.

windows_route.note: On _WIN32, GetIconThemeForDesktopEnvironment routes to the colibre / colibre_dark ids with no per-desktop routing (the elementary/breeze/sukapura desktop branches are #else only).

package_naming.note: Any future material theme must ship as images_material[_dark][_svg].zip to be discovered at all; the _svg/_dark suffixes are stripped for the display name.

rules.substring: RTL mirroring is applied per glyph
generated art is not accepted as runtime or evidence
rules.rule: no unreviewed generated art as evidence

rules.substring: theme for the native pipeline is specified here, not yet implemented
mirroring is semantic, not automatic for every glyph
Generated art is not accepted as

material_startcenter_glyphs.note: WIN-FND-007 inversion (same-commit triad with the Cluster F glyph authoring): the Material Start Center (sfx2/uiconfig/ui/startcenter.ui, drawn only behind IsMaterialStartCenterActive()) is the first surface to consume a bespoke Material glyph set. Every sfx2/res/startcenter/*.png icon-name it references is now REQUIRED-PRESENT and wired one of exactly two ways: (a) authored_svg -- a real 18px monochrome SVG (svg_fill #3a3a38) shipped in BOTH the colibre and colibre_svg trees, with NO links.txt alias, because a links.txt alias is consulted before the real file (ImplImageTree::getRealImageName) and would shadow the authored glyph; or (b) aliased -- NO authored file, a links.txt line reusing an existing colibre cmd icon (the _svg theme's links.txt is auto-derived .png->.svg by postprocess/CustomTarget_images.mk, so one colibre line serves both themes, and colibre_dark inherits via the fallback chain). authored_svg and aliased are disjoint and together close over the icon-names startcenter.ui references. This does NOT install a Material icon THEME: the assets live under existing colibre/colibre_svg, so material_theme_installed stays false and no material* theme directory exists. legacy_refs_absent flips the stock Start Center icon references to REQUIRED-ABSENT in startcenter.ui (migrate-never-delete). Source/text evidence only; runtime_verified:false -- no build, packed zip, rendered pixels, dark-tint, RTL, or scaling evidence is claimed.

icon_size_linter.note: A size linter for lc_/sc_ PNGs exists and walks the whole icon-themes tree (so it would cover any future material PNGs at 24/16px), but it is one of the stock upstream linters excluded from the Material validator gate: the script exists, CI does not currently enforce it in the Windows UI contract workflow.
